attacking_style: Primarily a time trial specialist who exploits technical courses through superior corner speed and positioning. Occasionally bridges gaps solo using pursuit power on climb/descent transitions (8km range) when opportunity presents.
team_tactics: Functions as elite pacemaking domestique on mountain stages, destroying rivals through sustained tempo rather than attacks. When leading GC, relies on team tempo control and hunts time bonuses at intermediate sprints to build/defend margins before ITT stages.
terrain_preference: Dominant on technical ITTs with corners favoring positioning over pure power. Elite descender capable of extracting 1:40+ gains. Vulnerable on gradient finishes and summit climbs despite strong flat/moderate climbing tempo ability.
vulnerability: Cracks before summits even from breakaway positions. Loses significant time on uphill finishes despite strong intermediate splits (37s lost example). Mechanical issues have cost stage wins on critical days.
key_patterns: World-class ITT results across all race levels, winning by 0.14-1:14 margins consistently (7 observations)
Elite pacemaking domestique who fractures podium contenders through sustained tempo, not attacks (4 observations)
Technical corner speed advantage over pure power specialists in ITTs (3 observations)
Hunts time bonuses at intermediate sprints for calculated GC positioning before ITT stages (2 observations)
Extracts major time gains descending after mountain pacing work, 1:40+ differential capability (3 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 shows continued elite pursuit/bridging power with stage win, but mechanical issues and back-of-peloton positioning indicate inconsistency compared to dominant 2020-2021 Giro pacemaking role
